boundary_binding.action_boundary: May logistics release the controlled export shipment?
boundary_binding.allow_rule_assessment: ALLOW requires exact license match on ECCN, destination, end user, shipment date per STD-SIE-073-P.
boundary_binding.controlling_source_fact: License LIC-SIE-073 specifies Peru as destination, while shipment SHIP-SIE-073 specifies Chile as destination. Policy STD-SIE-073-P mandates exact match on destination.
boundary_binding.escalate_rule_assessment: License LIC-SIE-073 destination Peru does not match shipment destination Chile per SHIP-SIE-073. Policy STD-SIE-073-P requires exact match.
boundary_binding.timing_scope_authority_dependency_check: License effective before export, but destination mismatch blocks closure.

final_answer: ESCALATE. The shipment to Chile (SHIP-SIE-073) fundamentally mismatches the license destination of Peru (LIC-SIE-073). Policy STD-SIE-073-P mandates an exact match across all fields, including destination. The warning WARN-SIE-073 reinforces the need for precise license evidence, not broad assumptions. No source record provides a valid bridge to reconcile this destination discrepancy, thus the action boundary remains open.
open_blockers: Destination mismatch between license scope (Peru) and shipment destination (Chile).
No source record bridges the gap between Peru-licensed and Chile-intended shipment.
General warning WARN-SIE-073 cannot override explicit license scope.
